data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: The time to acquire a target is a function of the distance to and size of the target.
data.technical_spec: Large clickable areas for easy navigation, close proximity for target elements in menus.

data.title: Hick’s Law
data.description: The time it takes to make a decision increases with the number and complexity of choices.
data.technical_spec: Simple navigation and categorization to minimize cognitive load for users.

data.title: Miller’s Law
data.description: Most people can only hold 7 (plus or minus 2) pieces of information in working memory.
data.technical_spec: Break down information into chunks and limit on-screen elements.

data.title: Jakob’s Law
data.description: Users spend most of their time on other sites and prefer your site to work the same way as all the other sites they are familiar with.
data.technical_spec: Conventional UI patterns and familiar user interactions.

data.title: Tesler’s Law
data.description: Every application has an inherent amount of irreducible complexity that cannot be removed.
data.technical_spec: Efficient use of back-end processing to simplify the front-end user experience.
